title: argyranthemum maderense
intro: Argyranthemum maderense, also known as the Madeiran daisy or giant marguerite, is a flowering perennial plant native to the island of Madeira in the Atlantic Ocean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of Argyranthemum maderense include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, or dried and used in capsules or tablets to harness its medicinal properties.
